codeReview.summary: TeamRegistry is well-implemented with 23 passing tests. The implementation correctly maps team IDs to task sets with DashMap for fine-grained concurrency, broadcasts TeamTaskFailed events to all team members when a task fails, and exposes all required operations (create_team, add_task_to_team, remove_task_from_team, disband_team, get_team_tasks). API design is clean with proper error handling via SwellError. Public re-exports are correct in lib.rs.

issues.description: Team struct uses std::sync::RwLock instead of tokio::RwLock. Since TeamRegistry itself is not async and Team is stored behind Arc, this is a minor inconsistency with the crate's async patterns, but not a bug since all Team operations are synchronous.

create_team() is marked async in the docstring example but is actually synchronous. The doc comment at line 28 shows `async fn create_team` but the implementation at line 125 is `pub fn create_team(&self, name: String) -> Uuid`. This is a documentation inconsistency.

summary: TeamRegistry implementation passes all checks. The feature correctly implements VAL-TASK-003: TeamRegistry maps team IDs to sets of task IDs with shared fate notification. When a task fails, TeamTaskFailed events are emitted to all remaining team members via broadcast channel. 23 tests cover team lifecycle, membership management, failure notifications, and concurrent access. The implementation is clean, thread-safe using DashMap, and properly integrated via re-exports in lib.rs. Two minor non-blocking issues: std::sync::RwLock vs tokio::RwLock inconsistency in Team struct (line 37), and a doc comment showing async create_team but the actual method is sync (line 28).
